## [00:00 - 03:02]

All right, welcome to the Meta ads training. Now Meta is the company formerly known as Facebook. You're going to notice a little bit of crossover here as they make the change over. You're seeing Meta on the screen, but in the URL, we've got Facebook. So in this video here, we're going to talk about setting up the business account, business manager. So you may already have this setup or you may not. If you don't, I don't see a lot of trainings actually show this from scratch. So here we go. You're just going to go to business.facebook.com. If you see this screen here, that means you don't currently have Meta business manager set up. If you see something different, you've likely already started that process or maybe it's already set up for you. But we're just going to click create an account and then you're going to type in your business name here. All right. Here, I've just put my name, but put your actual business name there, what you want it to be called, your name and then your email address. Hit submit. Then when you get to this screen, it's going to send you an email. You need to go there and click the link inside the email to activate the account. Okay. Once you have verified, you saw the little bubble in the bottom right corner here disappear. I click the link inside the email and it brings me to this screen. So this is the very best spot to manage your business. I see a lot of folks do it from their personal profile and that's not a great spot to do it. Reason being in here, there's a lot more flexibility, which I'll show you in a couple minutes here. So you can also take a tour, right? That's what this little box here is telling you. I'm just going to give you a tour that I consider a little bit more thorough and easier to follow. So we'll show later for this one. So in here, why this is important is you can add people in this account. If you do it through your personal profile, you can't add people or it's difficult to. So in here, you can add a person. If you're having a company help you with your ads, you can hit partners and then you can add a partner here. You can connect your pages here, your Facebook or Meta pages, your ad accounts, your Instagram account. That's the main accounts that you'll add. And then down here under data sources, if you click that, you're going to see pixels. We'll cover that in a little bit. You can do custom conversions. That's a popular tool we're going to use. And then payment methods. That's where you'll pay for your impressions, your Meta ads that you run. You'll pay for them through here by connecting a credit card. Down further here on business info, you're going to want to go into there and edit this thing for the actual legal name of the business, the address, the phone number.

## [03:02 - 05:59]

Everything you possibly can get in here, you want to enter in this area. This will become important if anything ever happens to your account. Maybe somebody gets into it that shouldn't. Hopefully that doesn't happen to you, but it is possible. So, doing this actual, these bits of information documented in here will be very much beneficial for you because if that happens, you can go and actually get a piece of paper that has your legal business name on it, submit it to Meta, and then they'll see you're the only rightful owner of this business and they'll unlock your account. That's when people skip over this and then run into trouble that they've trouble getting their accounts back. Now to go back a bit. We go on to people here. Why I say this part is important is because as you add things here, your ad accounts, your pages, your pixels, that kind of thing, when you enter another person in here, let's say I was to go add and then I put in their email address here, I can choose to give them employee access so they can only work on certain things or admin access to give them access to everything. As we add people here, you can actually add or take away rights for each person or remove the person altogether. ## [09:08 - 12:08]

Now you'll set up your ad account. See you going on down the left hand side here. We'll hit add accounts. And it says that we don't have any ad accounts. And now here, this is one spot that you can set up your people. You can do it over here on the left hand side a different way. I find it easier just to do it here as I'm going along. If you follow these videos, this is exactly how I set up new accounts. So for this person, myself, I get to have full control. Click assign. And then here you'll add payment info. I won't do that here because I'm going to do it later. But anyway, I'll show you how it's done. You click add payment info. And you simply verify all of this. And it does say here your location and currency cannot be changed once set. I live on a border town and I use US dollars and Canadian dollars. And so if that's you're in that sort of situation, you want to make sure you get it correct off the hop or you will not be able to change it. And then very simply in here, you just enter your choose what your payment method is going to be credit card or PayPal. Or if you have an I credit to claim, I usually skip all that and just go with the credit card. You can have these others later if you like. And then if you have a tax ID or your address, you can enter this in here. So very simply, I click next there. You just put your name in here, credit card number, expiry date, and then your security code and click save. Now a very, very important thing to note, this credit card that you use for these ads, you want to make sure that it has a credit card. A large enough limit to cover your entire ads spend each month. A really bad thing that can happen, not the end of the world, but very inconvenient is if you're running ads, they're doing really well. But your credit card has hit it's either its limit or the limit you set inside of Meta, then your ads will stop. Again, it's not the end of the world. You can always update the card and then go back and the ads will start up as soon as you make your payment. But I like to make sure that the credit card can support more than my ad spend. So I never ever run out of have my ads stop on me. It's not very convenient.

## [18:10 - 21:10]

And then you click domains. And then you go over here and click add. Create a new domain. And you put your domain name in here. And click add. That's literally all there is to it. This is very important though, because you are eventually you're going to set up your events and they're going to relate to this domain. So you have to have a clear idea of kind of the flow for your customer as they hit your website, your landing pages and so on. You need to have identified where that's going to take place. And I should point out that your domain can only be associated with one account. So if you own multiple businesses, you need to keep everything very, very clean. So for example, let's say your domain is bobsmith.com. So bobsmith.com can only be associated with this business manager, this pixel, this ad account, this Instagram. Everything needs to flow very cleanly and nicely. Now if Bob Smith also owned, let's say Bob Smith dental service, you'd have to have a completely different domain name for that.
